{"step_by_step_thinking": "Epigenetic modifications are heritable in certain contexts, particularly across generations in some organisms and under specific environmental conditions. Document [6] states that epigenetic modifications are 'heritable and stable changes in gene expression' that occur through alterations in the chromosome without changing the DNA sequence. Document [8] specifically mentions that environmental and nutritional factors can alter histones, leading to persistent changes that last a lifetime and can pass through paternal and maternal generations. This indicates transgenerational inheritance of epigenetic marks. Although the documents note that such modifications 'rarely remain through generations in humans', they also acknowledge that they can be passed on, especially in response to environmental exposures like trauma or nutrition. The evidence from studies on historical trauma and prenatal exposure supports the idea that epigenetic changes can be inherited across generations. Therefore, despite limitations in human transmission, the available evidence supports that epigenetic modifications can be heritable in some cases.", "answer_choice": "A"}
